name: Latent Illness and the Law: Filing for Benefits 10 Years After Your Last Day of Work
description: Latent Illness and the Law: Filing for Benefits 10 Years After Your Last Day of Work - St. George

step.name: Step 1
step.text: Discovering that a workplace exposure from years ago has caused a serious illness now is a devastating realization. Whether it’s cancer, heart disease, or chronic lung damage, the legal system has specific paths for these 'latent' injuries, but the window to act is narrow.

step.name: Step 3
step.text: The most critical step is the moment your doctor tells you that your illness is 'more likely than not' related to your previous work. In Utah and many other states, this is the 'Date of Discovery.' This date is what typically starts the statute of limitations clock. Ensure your doctor records this connection in your medical notes immediately.

step.name: Step 4
step.text: ### Step 2: Identify the Toxin and the Timeline

step.name: Step 5
step.text: You need to be able to name the substances you worked with.

step.name: Step 6
step.text: - Recover old W-2s or pay stubs to confirm exactly when you worked for the employer.

step.name: Step 7
step.text: - Research the chemicals commonly used in that industry during those years (e.g., benzene in refineries, asbestos in construction, or perchloroethylene in dry cleaning).

step.name: Step 8
step.text: - Write down the name of your old supervisor or coworkers who might still be able to testify about the conditions.

step.name: Step 10
step.text: Even if the company has changed names or relocated, you must attempt to notify them (or their current insurance carrier) of the claim. In Utah, the 'Last Injurious Exposure' rule means the last place you worked where that toxin was present is usually the one that has to pay.

step.name: Step 11
step.text: ### Step 4: File the Claim with the State Labor Commission

step.name: Step 12
step.text: Do not wait for the employer to 'do the right thing.' File your formal claim with the state workers' compensation board (such as the Utah Labor Commission). This creates a legal record of your demand for benefits.

step.name: Step 14
step.text: Latent illness cases are notoriously complex. Insurance companies will argue that the 'statute of repose' has expired or that your illness was caused by 'lifestyle factors' in the intervening years. At Just Work Comp Law, we specialize in tracking down old insurance policies and hiring industrial toxicologists to reconstruct old work environments.

step.name: Step 15
step.text: Timing is everything. If you wait even a few months after your diagnosis, you may lose your right to benefits forever. Contacting our office at 801-746-0290 immediately after a diagnosis is the smartest move you can make for your family's future.
